content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 15, 1986

Vigo G. Nielsen
Nielsen, Hodgson, Parrinello & Mueller
The Hartford Building
650 California Street, Suite 2650
San Francisco, CA 94108

Re: FPPC No. A-86-099

Dear Mr. Nielsen:

This is in reply to your letter dated March 10, 1986,
concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act. You asked for confirmation of two points
concerning campaign reports and statements which are sent by
"guaranteed overnight mail through the United States Postal
Service."

Gov. Code Sections 84203 and 84204 require that late
contributions and late independent expenditures be reported by:

mailgram, telegram, guaranteed overnight mail
through the United State Postal Service or
personal delivery within 24 hours.

Gov. Code Sections 84200.7, 84200.8 and 84202.5 require
that the second pre-election statement and "Supplemental
Pre-Election Statements" shall be delivered in person or "by
guaranteed overnight mail through the United States Postal
Service."

With regard to your specific questions, the Commission's
interpretation is as follows:

(1) Late contribution reports and late independent
expenditure reports which are sent by guaranteed overnight mail
through the United States Postal Service are required to be
sent within 24 hours of making or receiving the late
contribution or making the late independent expenditure. This
means that the report must be received by the United States
Postal Service within 24 hours of the time the contribution is
received or made or the expenditure is made.1/ The receipt

1/This applies, also, to reports which are filed by
mailgram or telegram; such reports must be received by Western
Union within 24 hours. Reports which are personally delivered,
however, must be received by the filing officer within 24 hours.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Vigo G. Nielsen
April 15, 1986
Page Two


provided by the United States Postal Service for guaranteed
overnight mail service is evidence that the 24-hour requirement
has been met.

(2) Guaranteed overnight delivery services such as Federal
Express, DHL, Airborne, etc., are not acceptable for filing
late contribution or late independent expenditure reports, or
for filing the second pre-election or "Supplemental"
pre-election statements. All reports which are sent by
guaranteed overnight delivery service must be sent through the
United States Postal Service.

Thank you for your inquiry.

Sincerely,

Jeanne Pritchard
Division Chief,
Technical Assistance and
Analysis
(916) 322-5662


JP:cah

LAW OFFICES OF

NIELSEN, HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

SACRAMENTO                                THE HARTFORD BUILDING
1030 FIFTEENTH STREET, SUITE 250         650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814              SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752                 TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800                FILE NUMBER

March 10, 1986




Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA  95814

                Re: Late Contribution Notices

Dear Jeanne:

     Because of the discussion at the FPPC's public seminar
on campaign disclosure held at the State Building in Oakland,
California on February 27, 1986, would you clarify the Commission's
position regarding the filing of late contribution notices?

     1.  Are late contribution and late expenditure notices
required to be sent within 24 hours?  Our understanding is that a
contributor or a recipient of a late contribution has 24 hours
from the time the contribution or expenditure is made or received
to put in the guaranteed overnight delivery service of the United
States Post Office a statement of such fact but that such notifica-
tion sent by guaranteed overnight mail service through the U.S.
Post Office need not be received by the filing official within 24
hours of sending or receipt.

     2.  Is it correct that other "guaranteed" overnight
delivery services such as Federal Express, DHL, Airborne, etc.
are not acceptable for filing late contribution or independent
expenditure notices nor are they acceptable for filing the
pre-election campaign statement?  Our understanding is that only
guaranteed overnight delivery through the United States Post
Office (Express Mail) is permitted to satisfy the timing based on
sending within the 24 hour deadline.

Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
March 10, 1986
Page 2


     If I have correctly stated the FPPC's position and you
wish to sign the bottom of this letter as confirmation, please
return it to me in the enclosed envelope.  If other clarification
is necessary, thank you in advance for your assistance.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        [signature]

                                        Vigo G. Nielsen, Jr.

VGN:bah

_____________________________
Jeanne Pritchard


_____________________________
Date

sections.question:
You asked for confirmation of two points concerning campaign reports and statements which are sent by "guaranteed overnight mail through the United states Postal Service."

(1) Are late contribution and late expenditure notices required to be sent within 24 hours? Our understanding is that a contributor or a recipient of a late contribution has 24 hours from the time the contribution or expenditure is made or received to put in the guaranteed overnight delivery service of the United States Post Office a statement of such fact but that such notification sent by guaranteed overnight mail service through the U. S. Post Office need not be received by the filing official within 24 hours of sending or receipt.

(2) Is it correct that other "guaranteed" overnight delivery services such as Federal Express, DHL, Airborne, etc. are not acceptable for filing late contribution or independent expenditure notices nor are they acceptable for filing the pre-election campaign statement?
sections.conclusion:
With regard to your specific questions, the Commission's interpretation is as follows:

(1) Late contribution reports and late independent expenditure reports which are sent by guaranteed overnight mail through the United states Postal service are required to be sent within 24 hours of making or receiving the late contribution or making the late independent expenditure. This means that the report must be received by the United states Postal Service within 24 hours of the time the contribution is received or made or the expenditure is made. The receipt provided by the united states Postal Service for guaranteed overnight mail service is evidence that the 24-hour requirement has been met.

(2) Guaranteed overnight delivery services such as Federal Express, DHL, Airborne, etc., are not acceptable for filing late contribution or late independent expenditure reports, or for filing the second pre-election or "Supplemental" pre-election statements. All reports which are sent by guaranteed overnight delivery service must be sent through the united states Postal Service.

sections.question_synthetic: The requester seeks FPPC confirmation on two points: (1) whether the 24-hour reporting deadline for late contributions and expenditures requires the report to be received by the filing officer within 24 hours or merely submitted to USPS overnight mail within that timeframe, and (2) whether only USPS guaranteed overnight mail is acceptable or if other overnight carriers like Federal Express are also compliant.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC clarified that the 24-hour deadline for late contribution and expenditure reports means the report must be received by USPS within 24 hours of the contribution or expenditure, with the USPS receipt serving as evidence of compliance. Only USPS guaranteed overnight mail is acceptable for such filings; other overnight carriers like Federal Express, DHL, and Airborne are not permitted.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document includes both incoming request (Nielsen's March 10, 1986 letter) and FPPC response (Pritchard's April 15, 1986 letter). Extracted primary question and conclusion from the FPPC response letter. Clear identification of two specific points being addressed. Minor typographical variations in document preserved as written (e.g., 'united states' vs 'United States').

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You asked for confirmation of two points concerning campaign reports and statements which are sent by "guaranteed overnight mail through the United states Postal Service."

(1) Are late contribution and late expenditure notices required to be sent within 24 hours? Our understanding is that a contributor or a recipient of a late contribution has 24 hours from the time the contribution or expenditure is made or received to put in the guaranteed overnight delivery service of the United States Post Office a statement of such fact but that such notification sent by guaranteed overnight mail service through the U. S. Post Office need not be received by the filing official within 24 hours of sending or receipt.

(2) Is it correct that other "guaranteed" overnight delivery services such as Federal Express, DHL, Airborne, etc. are not acceptable for filing late contribution or independent expenditure notices nor are they acceptable for filing the pre-election campaign statement?

CONCLUSION: With regard to your specific questions, the Commission's interpretation is as follows:

(1) Late contribution reports and late independent expenditure reports which are sent by guaranteed overnight mail through the United states Postal service are required to be sent within 24 hours of making or receiving the late contribution or making the late independent expenditure. This means that the report must be received by the United states Postal Service within 24 hours of the time the contribution is received or made or the expenditure is made. The receipt provided by the united states Postal Service for guaranteed overnight mail service is evidence that the 24-hour requirement has been met.

(2) Guaranteed overnight delivery services such as Federal Express, DHL, Airborne, etc., are not acceptable for filing late contribution or late independent expenditure reports, or for filing the second pre-election or "Supplemental" pre-election statements. All reports which are sent by guaranteed overnight delivery service must be sent through the united states Postal Service.

embedding.summary: FPPC Advice Letter A-86-099 addresses campaign disclosure requirements for late contributions and independent expenditures sent via guaranteed overnight mail, clarifying that such reports must reach USPS within 24 hours and that only USPS Express Mail is acceptable, not alternative overnight carriers.
